jokes.text: Because if it's quiet, it's literally pointless.
jokes.setup: Parent asks child to turn down loud video game
jokes.punchline: Child explains he literally cannot because quiet gaming is pointless
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven logic that captures modern gaming culture - the absurd but internally consistent reasoning of a kid

jokes.text: All you had to do was say please. / I said please. Twice! / Well, maybe you should have yelled it.
jokes.setup: Parent frustrated they said please twice but child didn't comply
jokes.punchline: Child suggests they should have yelled 'please' - missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation that captures the maddening logic of children who technically follow instructions while missing their spirit

jokes.text: Uh, hang on, my son's on fire.
jokes.setup: Neighbors checking on loud explosion, parent apologizing for noise
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions son is literally on fire as minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and economy - treating a catastrophic situation as a minor interruption is classic absurdist comedy

jokes.text: Kiffy, I wanna replace my wedding ring diamond with whatever this is.
jokes.setup: Family's apartment destroyed by meteorite, homeless and panicking
jokes.punchline: Partner focused on upgrading jewelry while family faces homelessness
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing misplaced priorities in crisis - captures materialistic mindset

jokes.text: Like Mars? Or Jesus?
jokes.setup: Professor explaining asteroid impacts on celestial objects
jokes.punchline: Equating Jesus with celestial bodies as impact targets
jokes.explanation: Sharp absurdist humor mixing scientific and religious concepts - economical and unexpected

jokes.text: Sorry. I thought this was an air fryer.
jokes.setup: Professor using sophisticated gas chromatograph for scientific analysis
jokes.punchline: Someone mistakes it for kitchen appliance and ruins the Manwich
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - confusion between high-tech science equipment and common kitchen appliance

jokes.text: Using this rock hammer I got from a Shawshank Redemption memorabilia website
jokes.setup: Professor needs scientific tool for rock analysis
jokes.punchline: Using movie memorabilia instead of actual scientific equipment
jokes.explanation: Clever blend of pop culture and scientific process - Professor's questionable equipment choices

jokes.text: I thought it smelled familiar.
jokes.setup: Rock identified as being from Zoidberg's home planet
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg casually recognizes home planet by smell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his alien perspective delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Alas, even though you're immensely poor and that tiny sliver I burned would've made you a millionaire, it rightfully belongs to your whole civilization.
jokes.setup: Family excited about potential wealth from meteorite
jokes.punchline: Professor crushes dreams by explaining the rock belongs to Zoidberg's species
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Professor's tactless delivery of devastating news to poor family

jokes.text: That raises an interesting question. / Yes? / What are you doing later?
jokes.setup: Scientist about to ask scientific question about rock origin
jokes.punchline: Actually asking for a date instead
jokes.explanation: Well-executed misdirection - builds expectation for scientific revelation, delivers pickup line

jokes.text: Sorry, you're not my type. / I had to give it a shot. / You actually didn't.
jokes.setup: Character gets rejected after inappropriate pickup line
jokes.punchline: Dr. Judith points out the attempt was completely unnecessary
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that deflates the 'had to try' excuse with simple logic

jokes.text: So how'd the rock get to Earth? I mean, it didn't just walk. Right? Back me up, guys.
jokes.setup: Obvious question about how a rock traveled through space
jokes.punchline: Character needs validation that rocks don't walk
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment - character overthinking obvious concepts and seeking support

jokes.text: Before Crab.
jokes.setup: Explaining timeline as '5 billion B.C.'
jokes.punchline: B.C. means 'Before Crab' instead of 'Before Christ' on crab planet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant worldbuilding pun - perfectly logical for crab civilization's dating system

jokes.text: Wow. I feel so special! / You're not.
jokes.setup: Character excited about rare meteorite hitting their building
jokes.punchline: Dr. Judith immediately deflates their specialness
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation - brutal efficiency in crushing someone's moment of feeling special

jokes.text: Marry me. / That's not a question. / Marry me?
jokes.setup: Character makes romantic declaration as statement
jokes.punchline: When corrected about grammar, adds question mark without changing meaning
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective grammar joke - character misses point about appropriateness, focuses on punctuation

jokes.text: It's, like, we get it. They were big animals.
jokes.setup: Explanation about endless Crabassic Park movie franchise
jokes.punchline: Character dismisses entire franchise premise as repetitive
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about franchise fatigue - applies to real-world movie sequels

jokes.text: Decapod 10 is nice, but it smells like a clam's armpit.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg commenting on his home planet
jokes.punchline: Specific and disgusting comparison for planetary odor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg line - specific, gross, and delivered with characteristic bluntness about his own planet

jokes.text: We won't.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg says he'll be in dumpster if they need him
jokes.punchline: Immediate, blunt confirmation they won't need him
jokes.explanation: Brutal economy - two words that perfectly capture the group's attitude toward Zoidberg

jokes.text: Sorry about the trash. Apparently, the previous tenant was kind of a pig.
jokes.setup: New tenants moved into Zoidberg's former dumpster home
jokes.punchline: Calling Zoidberg a pig while literally living in garbage
jokes.explanation: Good ironic situation - family judging Zoidberg's cleanliness while moving into a dumpster

jokes.text: It has running water? / It has running something.
jokes.setup: New tenants excited about dumpster having running water
jokes.punchline: Landlord's ominous clarification suggests it's not water
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - lets imagination fill in what's actually running through the pipes

jokes.text: ¿Quién es Bender? / Uh, I'm late for a gynecologist appointment! / Me too! Let's carpool.
jokes.setup: Bender pretending not to know himself in Spanish to avoid helping
jokes.punchline: Amy and Leela's increasingly desperate excuses to avoid taking Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Good escalating desperation - characters making increasingly ridiculous excuses to avoid responsibility

jokes.text: Porque no quiero Zoidberg to live with us!
jokes.setup: Fry asks why Bender is kicking him after agreeing to let Zoidberg stay
jokes.punchline: Bender explains in Spanish-English mix that he doesn't want Zoidberg either
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reveals Bender's continued deception while expressing his true feelings

jokes.text: How dare he! That's my move!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg has trashed the apartment
jokes.punchline: Bender is offended because apartment-trashing is his signature behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Bender being territorial about destructive behavior is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Your Jacuzzi is wonderful! Those jets really get into your crevices.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg enjoying amenities at Fry/Bender's place
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed and gross description of dishwasher experience
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg grossness - mistaking dishwasher for jacuzzi with TMI details

jokes.text: By seeing whether your tentacle is covering the screen again. / Oh, it is.
jokes.setup: Munda can't see Leela and asks how to check if tentacle is covering screen
jokes.punchline: Obvious solution delivered as revelation
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy about video call problems - relatable modern tech humor

jokes.text: I never had a room. / You guys gave me up for adoption. Remember? / Well, it's the room you would've had.
jokes.setup: Mother talking about Leela's empty room waiting for her
jokes.punchline: Deflecting abandonment guilt with hypothetical room logic
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that addresses serious family dysfunction with absurd logic - well-crafted emotional core

jokes.text: God, she looked awful.
jokes.setup: Mom thinking Leela hung up
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting daughter's appearance when thinking she can't hear
jokes.explanation: Perfect toxic parent moment - immediate backbiting when thinking unheard

jokes.text: What do you call it? / A roll of paper towels. / Mm! And the bone is the best part.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg praising Munda's cooking
jokes.punchline: He's literally eating paper towels and thinks the cardboard tube is a bone
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - Zoidberg's alien palate making inedible items seem gourmet, with perfect escalation to the 'bone'

jokes.text: Who was it? / Nobody. With no cookies.
jokes.setup: Munda returns from door after neighbor brought cookies
jokes.punchline: Lying to Morris while emphasizing the lack of cookies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Munda's selfishness - denying Morris even knowledge of cookies

jokes.text: You cancelling's become part of the tradition.
jokes.setup: Leela apologizing for not being able to join family trip
jokes.punchline: Mother casually acknowledging daughter's consistent abandonment as normal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark family dysfunction humor - turning disappointment into tradition is both funny and deeply sad

jokes.text: That? That's just her ugly profile photo.
jokes.setup: Morris thinks he can still see Leela on screen
jokes.punchline: Munda casually insults daughter's appearance while explaining technology
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - mother's casual cruelty mixed with tech confusion creates uncomfortable laugh

jokes.text: Beach Boys 'I Get Around' performance
jokes.setup: Family bonding moment during karaoke/performance
jokes.punchline: Elaborate Beach Boys performance in sewer setting
jokes.explanation: Extended musical sequence - visual comedy but goes on too long

jokes.text: Even though it's not.
jokes.setup: Parents saying they've worked hard to make Zoidberg feel at home
jokes.punchline: Leela's bitter aside that it's literally not his home
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Leela's jealousy expressed through pedantic correction

jokes.text: He's a treasure, is what he is.
jokes.setup: Parents describing how much they love Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Calling obviously gross Zoidberg a 'treasure' with genuine affection
jokes.explanation: Parents' genuine love for objectively awful houseguest

jokes.text: When my species mates, they die, so I never met my parents. I never got to experience their love, or abuse, whichever one they were gonna do.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg explaining why this family means so much to him
jokes.punchline: Casually equating parental love with abuse as equally possible outcomes
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - treating parental abuse as a 50/50 possibility while delivering genuine emotion. Perfect Zoidberg character integration.

jokes.text: Well, we won't make that mistake again.
jokes.setup: Leela pointing out they gave her up for adoption
jokes.punchline: Father calling giving her up a 'mistake' they won't repeat
jokes.explanation: Dark family comedy - father accidentally confirming giving away daughter was an error while committing to new 'son'

jokes.text: Sibling rivalry, I love it!
jokes.setup: Leela upset about Zoidberg being adopted as brother
jokes.punchline: Mom delighted by daughter's obvious pain over replacement
jokes.explanation: Mom's oblivious enjoyment of daughter's emotional distress

jokes.text: You're not my brother! You're a gross, lazy, freeloading bum! / Sibling rivalry, I love it!
jokes.setup: Leela exploding with anger at Zoidberg and the situation
jokes.punchline: Father interpreting genuine hatred as cute sibling dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good misinterpretation comedy - parent's obliviousness to daughter's genuine distress

jokes.text: Just sign your John Gamecock here, here, and, uh, fill in your young'un's date of hatchin'. P'caw!
jokes.setup: Official adoption paperwork being processed
jokes.punchline: Southern rooster clerk using chicken terminology for legal documents
jokes.explanation: Solid character voice joke - mixing legal formality with barnyard terminology

jokes.text: That debt was supposed to be mine! / I can't believe I'll have my own debt someday. I promise to nurture and grow it.
jokes.setup: Leela upset about losing inheritance of family debt
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg treating debt like a cherished family heirloom to cultivate
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - treating debt as something precious to inherit and develop. Perfect character contrast.

jokes.text: The dumpster's on the small side, but it's a good school district. I always say, 'location, location, location.'
jokes.setup: Johnson family moved into dumpster
jokes.punchline: Applying real estate clichés to literal garbage dwelling
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of real estate speak with garbage living reality

jokes.text: Where do you live? / The sewer.
jokes.setup: Real estate agent giving standard location advice
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to living in literal sewage system
jokes.explanation: Simple deadpan delivery that punctures real estate clichés with harsh reality

jokes.text: You're stealing my parents! / You mean our parents.
jokes.setup: Leela accusing Zoidberg of taking her parents away
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's correction emphasizes the adoption makes them siblings
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of family tension - Zoidberg's innocent correction makes Leela's anger more painful

jokes.text: I wish we never abandoned you!
jokes.setup: Dad angry at Leela for being terrible daughter
jokes.punchline: Wishing they'd never abandoned her (instead of wishing they'd raised her)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - parent's regret about abandonment is wrong direction entirely

jokes.text: I wish we never abandoned you!
jokes.setup: Mother getting angry at Leela for being terrible daughter
jokes.punchline: Wishing she'd never committed the original abandonment
jokes.explanation: Brutally dark family comedy - mother wishing she'd kept daughter just to continue punishing her

jokes.text: Different doctor! Different doctor!
jokes.setup: Someone suggests their son is a doctor when Leela needs medical help
jokes.punchline: Panicked clarification that they need a real doctor, not Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy - family knows Zoidberg's medical incompetence

jokes.text: Do you mind holding my hand, sir? / Of course.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg about to deliver bad medical news
jokes.punchline: Doctor seeking emotional support from patient's father
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg character moment - incompetent doctor needing comfort while delivering diagnosis

jokes.text: I'm afraid... you're stupid.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg using dramatic 'I'm afraid' medical delivery pattern
jokes.punchline: Instead of medical diagnosis, just calls someone stupid
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection using medical drama format for simple insult - perfect Zoidberg incompetence

jokes.text: But-- but-- How can Zoidberg be related to me? / It defies explanation! / I can explain it!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg dramatically claims the relationship defies explanation
jokes.punchline: Dr. Judith immediately offers to explain it
jokes.explanation: Good comedic timing - Zoidberg's melodrama immediately deflated by practical scientist

jokes.text: Yes. Did you receive my restraining order?
jokes.setup: Character asking if she received his marriage proposal
jokes.punchline: Her counter-question about legal action against him
jokes.explanation: Strong callback that escalates the rejection from earlier - perfect deadpan escalation

jokes.text: ...jazzing them up with claws and tentacles... Woop-woop-woop-woop-woop-woop.
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation of how crab DNA spread to other planets
jokes.punchline: Professional scientist doing Zoidberg's signature sound effect
jokes.explanation: Decent callback - serious scientist momentarily adopting Zoidberg's mannerisms during explanation

jokes.text: And I thought you were just a vapid hunk.
jokes.setup: Professor shows scientific knowledge
jokes.punchline: Dr. Judith's backhanded compliment about his looks vs. intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of stereotypical scientist appearance assumptions

jokes.text: Use one of those.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg offering to donate blenal gland but asking if he'll be okay
jokes.punchline: Pointing to multiple glands hanging out of his body
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy - Zoidberg's alien anatomy casually solving the medical crisis

jokes.text: If I die, please donate my debt to a children's hospital.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg going into surgery
jokes.punchline: Wants to donate debt (not organs) to children's hospital
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to debt excitement - treating debt as charitable gift to children

jokes.text: Why don't they just spray us with a tea fire hose?
jokes.setup: Parents being overly attentive with pillows and tea
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's absurd escalation of their hovering behavior
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - taking parental over-attention to ridiculous extreme

jokes.text: We found a refrigerator box that's twice the size of this place! You get a lot more box for your buck out there.
jokes.setup: Johnsons moving from dumpster
jokes.punchline: Real estate language applied to cardboard box shopping
jokes.explanation: Good callback to real estate language for homeless living situations

jokes.text: We'll work somethin' out. / Okay. As long as it doesn't involve money.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg needs housing but has no money
jokes.punchline: Agreeing to arrangement as long as it doesn't involve the thing he doesn't have
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Zoidberg's poverty forcing him to accept any terms except the impossible
